stories.title: Alabama Earns No. 1 Overall Seed — First Time in 16 Years, Despite Losing SEC Final to Texas

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Alabama is the No. 1 overall seed in the 2026 NCAA Softball Tournament. Yes — even after losing to Texas 7-1 in the SEC title game. A 49-7 record, 22 shutouts, and the nation's 2nd-best ERA lock in the top spot. First No. 1 seed in 16 years. #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The committee's message: one loss doesn't define a 49-7 season. Alabama beat Top 10 opponents in back-to-back tournament days before the title game. The No. 1 seed was theirs before Saturday's final even started. Body of work wins. #RollTide #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: SEC Lands 6 of Top 8 Seeds — Conference Dominance Reshapes Entire Bracket

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: SEC softball just took over the bracket. Six of the top 8 national seeds are from the SEC: Alabama (1), Texas (2), Oklahoma (3), Arkansas (5), Florida (6), Tennessee (7). The conference is not done. #CollegeSoftball #SECSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: The SEC put 12 of its 15 programs into the 2026 NCAA Softball Tournament field. That's not dominance — that's a takeover. Six of the top 8 seeds. All the top four host sites are in the SEC footprint. #CollegeSoftball #SECSoftball

stories.title: Oklahoma Is No. 3 Seed — Sooners' Historic Offense Earns Home Seed, Norman Regional Set

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Oklahoma earned the No. 3 national seed and hosts the Norman Regional. The Sooners enter the tournament with the nation's best batting average (.389) and 174 team home runs. 48-8 on the season. The most dangerous offense in college softball is hosting. #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: 2026 NCAA Softball Regionals Preview — 16 Sites, All Plays Start Friday May 15

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: 16 regional sites. 64 teams. The road to OKC starts Friday. 2026 NCAA Softball Tournament regionals open May 15-17 at all 16 host sites. Top seeds at home: Alabama (Tuscaloosa), Texas (Austin), Oklahoma (Norman), Nebraska (Lincoln). Let's go. #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Texas Is No. 2 Seed, Hosts Austin Regional — SEC Champions Rewarded With Home Field

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Texas is the No. 2 national seed and hosts the Austin Regional. After winning the first SEC Tournament title in program history, the Longhorns get to play May 15-17 in front of their home crowd. Texas vs. Wagner | Wisconsin vs. Baylor. #HookEm #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Nebraska's No. 4 Seed — Big Ten Champions Host Lincoln Regional After Historic Season

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: Nebraska is the No. 4 national seed. The Huskers go 45-6, win the Big Ten title, host a WCWS record home-run moment from Megan Grant — and earn home games in Lincoln to start NCAA play. Big Ten's best is ready. #Huskers #CollegeSoftball
